channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Exploring the Legacy of Flying Aces Magazine's 1930s Covers
item.description: Flying Aces was a prominent pulp magazine during the Golden Age of Aviation, published from 1928 to the 1940s. It captivated readers with thrilling tales of daring pilots, aerial combat, and heroic ex...

item.title: US and Iran escalate clashes as Strait of Hormuz traffic plummets
item.description: The article details the escalating conflict between the United States and Iran, with both nations engaging in military strikes that have significantly disrupted maritime traffic through the Strait of ...

item.title: Parliamentary Inquiry Calls for Oversight of DPP Amid Controversial Findings
item.description: A NSW parliamentary inquiry has recommended establishing a new oversight committee for the Office of the Director of Public Prosecutions (ODPP) following a controversial report alleging the DPP, Sally...

item.title: Cape Town mass shootings result in seven fatalities, police enhance security
item.description: Two mass shootings in Cape Town have left seven people dead and five others injured, according to provincial police. The first incident occurred at around 8:10pm on Wednesday at a tuck shop in Lost Ci...

item.title: Prince William Plays Crazy Golf in Hastings, Scores Well Among Celebrities
item.description: Prince William participated in a casual game of crazy golf at Hastings Adventure Golf, where he competed against celebrities like comedian Tim Vine and presenter Fern Britton. Despite not achieving a ...

item.title: Tanzania's Sh200 Billion Youth Loan Scheme Aims to Empower Young Entrepreneurs and Boost Employment
item.description: The Tanzanian government has launched a Sh200 billion youth loan program through the Youth Development Fund, targeting young entrepreneurs to stimulate economic growth and create jobs. Minister Joel N...

item.title: Analysis of Bonnie Tyler's death and media clickbait tactics
item.description: The article discusses the passing of Bonnie Tyler, a renowned singer, and critiques the sensationalist nature of the original title. It highlights how the title's dramatic phrasing, such as 'clickbait...

item.title: Canadian Smuggler Sentenced to 20 Years for Cocaine and Meth Trafficking
item.description: Guramrit Sidhu, a Canadian national dubbed 'The King' in underworld circles, was sentenced to 20 years in U.S. prison for orchestrating large-scale cocaine and methamphetamine smuggling operations int...

item.title: Jen Psaki Warns of Trump's Dangerous Desperation and Political Risks
item.description: In a recent episode of her MS NOW show, former White House press secretary Jen Psaki addressed the growing public mockery of President Donald Trump, cautioning that excessive laughter at his missteps ...

item.title: France Advance to World Cup Semi-Finals with 2-0 Win Over Morocco
item.description: France booked their place in the 2026 FIFA World Cup semi-finals with a 2-0 victory over Morocco in the quarter-final at Foxborough, Massachusetts. Kylian Mbappé opened the scoring in the 60th minute ...

item.title: Philippines Honors South Korean Envoy with Highest Diplomatic Award
item.description: President Ferdinand 'Bongbong' Marcos Jr. conferred the Order of Sikatuna, the Philippines' highest diplomatic honor, on outgoing South Korean Ambassador Lee Sang-hwa during his farewell call at Malac...

item.title: 23 Arrested for Incitement and Conspiracy During Saba Saba Celebrations in Mwanza
item.description: Police in Mwanza Region have arrested 23 individuals accused of incitement, conspiracy to commit violence, and other criminal offenses linked to events surrounding Saba Saba celebrations on July 7, 20...

item.title: Discrepancy in Time of Death Certificate for Ateneo Student Baterbonia
item.description: The Philippine National Police’s Criminal Investigation and Detection Group (PNP-CIDG) noted a discrepancy between the time of death indicated in the death certificate of Ateneo student Rene Baterboni...

item.title: Nuclear Disarmament and Climate Action: A Global Imperative
item.description: The blog post from July 9, 2026, highlights the intersection of nuclear disarmament, climate change, and environmental justice. It discusses ongoing efforts to address nuclear waste, promote renewable...

item.title: Hayley Wickenheiser leaves Maple Leafs after eight seasons in front office role
item.description: Hayley Wickenheiser, a hockey legend with a storied career, has parted ways with the Toronto Maple Leafs after eight seasons in the team's front office. The decision, announced by new general manager ...

item.title: Exploring the Complex Realities of Motherhood in Bollywood's New Play
item.description: Bollywood actor Kalki Koechlin and theatre director Sheena Khalid's play 'Belly of the Beast' confronts the often-overlooked challenges of motherhood, from postpartum depression and miscarriage to soc...

item.title: France defeat Morocco 2-0, reach World Cup semi-finals with Mbappe's goal
item.description: France secured a 2-0 victory over Morocco in their World Cup quarter-final clash at Boston Stadium, advancing to the semi-finals. Kylian Mbappe, the star striker, recovered from a missed penalty in th...

item.title: Melbourne's Last Miznon Restaurant Closes After Nearly a Decade
item.description: The Israeli street-food chain Miznon has announced the closure of its Melbourne CBD branch, marking the end of an almost 10-year chapter for the city's only remaining location. The decision comes afte...

item.title: BSNL's Satellite Phone for Specialized Use: Features and Purchase Guidelines
item.description: Bharat Sanchar Nigam Limited (BSNL) has introduced a satellite phone priced at ₹1.34 lakh, targeting specialized sectors like defense, maritime operations, disaster response, and remote areas. The dev...

item.title: Uganda Government Supports Repatriated Citizens with National IDs and Reintegration Programmes
item.description: The Ugandan government is moving swiftly to issue National Identity Cards to over 700 citizens who were recently repatriated from South Africa following xenophobic attacks. This initiative, led by the...
